topic: A person learns their childhood bully became a therapist
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sterile scent of the office always made my stomach clench. I sat across from him, a man whose tailored suit seemed to absorb all the available light. His name was Mark, and the very sound of it brought a tight knot to my chest. I kept my hands folded tightly on the edge of my worn notepad, refusing to meet his gaze. Every time he shifted in his expensive leather chair, I felt a prickle of irritation crawl up my neck. He spoke with a calm, measured cadence, dissecting my anxieties as if they were specimens under a microscope. I wanted to snap back, to tell him he was wrong, but the words caught in my throat, replaced by a dull, simmering resentment that radiated off me in palpable tension. I focused intently on a smudge on the table, willing myself to become invisible.

[story 2]

I stood by the door, my knuckles white where I gripped the frame. The air in the waiting room felt thick, suffocating. When Mark finally emerged, he looked… different. The familiar smirk was gone, replaced by an unnerving gentleness that made my jaw tighten. He offered a small, reassuring nod, and the gesture felt like a deliberate dismissal of everything I felt. "Come in, Sarah," he said, his voice soft, almost melodic. I shuffled forward, my steps heavy and reluctant. My shoulders were hunched up around my ears, a physical manifestation of the resistance churning inside me. I kept my eyes fixed on the floor tiles, cataloging every tiny imperfection, a silent declaration that I was utterly unimpressed by this performance of empathy.

[story 3]

The silence in the room was a deliberate weapon. Mark leaned forward, his expression one of intense, focused attention, and I felt a distinct hardening in my chest. I watched the way his eyes tracked my responses, searching for the crack in my composure. I answered his probing questions with clipped, precise sentences, each word carefully chosen to convey distance. A strange, uncomfortable heat began to bloom behind my eyes, a sharp, unpleasant sensation that I fought to suppress. I wanted to challenge the premise of the conversation, to dismantle his carefully constructed façade, but I found myself merely nodding, a slow, deliberate movement that communicated nothing but profound disagreement and a deep-seated refusal to be managed.
